title: Ensure MFA is enabled for the "root" account

input_row.title: Ensure MFA is enabled for the "root" account
input_row.description: With MFA enabled, anytime the “root” account logs on to Alibaba Cloud, it will be prompted for username and password followed by an authentication code from the virtual MFA device. It is recommended that MFA be enabled for the “root” user.
input_row.rationale: It is important to prevent “root” account from being compromised. Enabling MFA requires the “root” account holder to provide additional information on top of username and password. When MFA is enabled, an attacker faces at least two different authentication mechanisms. The additional security makes it harder for an attacker to gain access to protected resources or data.
input_row.audit: Perform the following to determine if an MFA device is enabled for the “root” account: Through the management console: 1. Logon to RAM console by using your Alibaba Cloud account (root account). 2. In the left-side navigation pane, click Overview. 3. In the Security Check section, make sure that Enable MFA for Root Account is marked as Finished.
input_row.remediation: Perform the following to enable MFA for “root” account Through the management console: 1. Logon to RAM console by using your Alibaba Cloud account (root account). 2. Move the pointer over the account icon in the upper-right corner and click Security Settings. 3. In the Account Protection section, Click Edit. 4. On the displayed page, select a scenario and select TOTP. 5. Click Submit. 6. On the displayed page, click Verify now. 18 | P a g e 7. Enter the verification code and click Submit. 8. Download and install a mobile application that supports TOTP MFA, such as Google Authenticator, on your mobile phone. Note: If you already installed Google Authenticator, click Next. o For iOS: Install Google Authenticator from the App Store. o For Android: Install Google Authenticator from the Google Play Store. Note: You need to install a QR code scanner from the Google Play Store for Google Authenticator to identify QR codes. 9. After you install Google Authenticator, go back to the Identity Verification page and click Next. 10. Open Google Authenticator and tap BEGIN SETUP. o Tap Scan barcode and scan the QR code on the Identity Verification page. o Tap Manual entry, enter the username and key, and then tap the check mark (√) icon. Note: You can obtain the username and key by moving the pointer over Scan failed on the Identity Verification page. 11. On the Identity Verification page, enter the 6-digit verification code obtained from Google Authenticator and click Next. Note: The verification code is refreshed at an interval of 30 seconds.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**PROGRAM NAME:** ali_ram_account_mfa_enabled

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:** 
The automated program will check whether Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) is enabled for the root account in Alibaba Cloud. The following actions will be taken:

1. **API Calls:**
   - Use the Alibaba Cloud API method `DescribeMFADevice` from the RAM (Resource Access Management) service to evaluate the MFA device status for the root account. This method checks if an MFA device is associated with the root account.

2. **Aliyun CLI Commands:**
   - A possible Aliyun CLI command: `aliyun ram DescribeMFADevices --UserName <root_account_username>`. This command retrieves the MFA device status associated with the specified username.

3. **Resources to Enumerate:**
   - The audit will specifically target the root account of the Alibaba Cloud organization.

4. **Configuration Properties/Settings to Validate:**
   - The returned response from the API/CLI needs to confirm if a valid MFA device is returned. A valid MFA device indicates that MFA is enabled.

5. **Pass/Fail Criteria:**
   - **Pass:** If the API response contains details of an MFA device for the root account, indicating MFA is enabled.
   - **Fail:** If the API response indicates that no MFA device is configured for the root account.

6. **Example Criteria:** 
   - Check if the list in the `MFADevices` returned by the API is not empty or if the attribute `UserName` for the root account exists with corresponding device details.

This automated approach ensures a quick verification of the MFA status for the root account without manual intervention, thereby improving compliance monitoring and security posture.
